brandSub: Protocol · Diplomacy · Public Communication · Security · Communicology
metaDesc: Author guidelines for WPA Journal: scope, article types, format, ethics, AI-use disclosure and submission.
title: Author Guidelines
deck: Everything you need to prepare and submit a manuscript to WPA Journal.
k002: WPA Journal publishes scholarly and professional work in protocol, diplomacy, public communication, security studies, communicology, cultural diplomacy, institutional communication and protocol scenarios.
k012: WPA Journal accepts manuscripts in Macedonian and English. Titles, abstracts and keywords are encouraged in both languages where possible.
k014: Submit an editable document. A typical structure includes title, author and affiliation, abstract, keywords, main text with clear sections, conclusion, and references.
k016: Abstract: approximately 150–250 words. Keywords: 4–8 terms describing the core topics of the article.
k018: Use a consistent, recognised academic citation style throughout the manuscript. Every reference must be verifiable; do not include unverified or fabricated bibliographic data.
k020: Authors are encouraged to provide an ORCID identifier to support unambiguous attribution.
k022: AI tools may assist with language, structure or research support. Authors remain fully responsible for the accuracy of all content. Any meaningful use of AI must be disclosed.
k024: Authors must follow research and publication ethics and disclose any conflicts of interest. Fabricated citations and false attribution are strictly prohibited.
k026: Submissions must be original and not under simultaneous review elsewhere. Plagiarism in any form is not accepted.
k028: All submissions undergo editorial screening for scope, quality, citation integrity and ethics.
k029: The review model is being activated gradually and will be clearly stated for each issue or article.
k031: Authors retain copyright. The default license is Creative Commons BY-NC-ND 4.0 unless otherwise agreed.
k033: During the preparation phase, manuscripts are submitted by email to worldprotocolacademy@gmail.com. See the Submit Your Paper page for the required information.
k034: WPA Journal is an independent institutional publication platform. External indexing, ISSN, DOI and other bibliographic statuses are listed only when officially obtained.
